design_philosophy.target_user: English-speaking expats in Vietnam who see Cờ tướng played at cafés and want to join in
design_philosophy.session_length: Under 10 minutes per lesson — coffee-shop-sized learning
design_philosophy.core_principle: Progressive piece introduction with mini-games, not full-game-first
pedagogical_basis: Andragogy (Knowles): Adults learn best when they understand WHY and can immediately apply knowledge to real-world situations
Spaced repetition (Ebbinghaus): Puzzles from earlier lessons resurface in later sessions to combat the forgetting curve
Mini-game pedagogy: Isolate one or two piece types per lesson with a clear win condition before combining
Three-layer labeling: Character on the disc → Vietnamese name they'll hear → English explanation
design_philosophy.completion_goal: An expat can work through all 7 core lessons in a week of café visits and sit down to play a real game by the weekend

piece_reference.description: All pieces with their three-layer labels. Red and Black use different characters on the physical discs.

pieces.note: Gains sideways movement after crossing the river — value increases to ~2

lessons.title: The Chariot — Your Strongest Warrior
lessons.café_context: Look at the board next to you. The round discs marked 俥 or 車 are Xe — Chariots. They're the most powerful pieces on the board, and they move exactly like a Rook in Western chess.
learning_objectives: Recognize 俥 (red) and 車 (black) characters on physical discs
Understand Xe moves any number of squares along a rank or file
Understand Xe cannot jump over pieces
Learn that pieces are placed on intersections, not inside squares

content.name: Xe — Chariot
content.movement_diagram: Highlight all reachable intersections from center of empty board along rank and file
content.real_world_tip: If you know chess, this is your Rook. Strongest piece on the board.
content.cultural_note: Xe also means 'vehicle' in Vietnamese. You'll see it on street signs — xe máy (motorbike), xe buýt (bus).

try_it.title: Chariot Capture Race

setup.board: Empty board with 2 red Xe vs 2 black Xe
setup.scattered_targets: 6 neutral tokens placed on random intersections

try_it.objective: Capture more tokens than your opponent (AI). First to 4 wins.
rules: Standard Xe movement (any number of squares along rank or file)
Capture a token by landing on its intersection
Xe can also capture opponent's Xe
Alternate turns
try_it.ai_difficulty: Makes random legal moves with slight preference for tokens

puzzles.title: Chariot's Reach
puzzles.prompt: Tap all intersections the red Xe can reach in one move.
puzzles.setup: Red Xe on e5 with 3 blocking pieces on the board

puzzles.title: Capture in One
puzzles.prompt: The black Xe is unprotected. Capture it in one move.
puzzles.setup: Red Xe on a1, Black Xe on a7, no obstacles

puzzles.title: Blocked Path
puzzles.prompt: Can the red Xe capture the black Xe? Find the path.
puzzles.setup: Red Xe on a1, blocking piece on a4, Black Xe on a8
puzzles.teaches: Xe cannot jump over pieces

puzzles.title: Fork the Tokens
puzzles.prompt: Move the Xe to an intersection where it threatens two enemy pieces at once.
puzzles.setup: Red Xe on e1, Black pieces on a5 and e9

puzzles.title: Escape Route
puzzles.prompt: Your Xe is under attack. Move it to safety while also threatening an enemy piece.
puzzles.setup: Red Xe on d5 threatened by Black Xe on d9, Black Ma on h5

lessons.lesson_summary: You now know the strongest piece in Cờ tướng. Next time you see 俥 or 車 on the board at the café, you know what it does.

lessons.title: The Horse — Jumps, But Watch Its Legs
lessons.café_context: The discs marked 傌 or 馬 are Mã — Horses. They jump in an L-shape like a chess Knight, but there's a catch: if a piece is adjacent to the Horse blocking its first step, it can't jump that direction.
learning_objectives: Recognize 傌 (red) and 馬 (black) characters
Understand L-shaped movement (one step orthogonal, then one step diagonal outward)
Understand the leg-block rule: a piece adjacent in the orthogonal direction blocks that jump
Identify when a Horse is blocked vs free

content.name: Mã — Horse
content.movement_diagram: Show all 8 possible destinations from center, with blocking squares highlighted in red
content.real_world_tip: Like a chess Knight, but it CAN be blocked. This is the first big difference you'll notice from Western chess.
content.cultural_note: Mã appears in the Vietnamese zodiac — ngựa/mã. The Horse is associated with energy and adventure.

try_it.title: Horse Obstacle Course

setup.board: Red Ma on a1, target intersection on i9, 8 blocking pieces scattered on board

try_it.objective: Navigate your Horse from corner to corner in the fewest moves, avoiding blocked paths.
rules: Standard Ma movement with leg-block rule enforced
Blocked jumps are shown with a red indicator
Count your moves — par is displayed after completion
try_it.success_criteria: Player reaches target intersection. Bonus for under-par.

puzzles.title: Where Can It Jump?
puzzles.prompt: Tap all intersections this Horse can legally reach.
puzzles.setup: Red Ma on e5 with 2 adjacent blocking pieces
puzzles.teaches: Identifying legal vs blocked jumps

puzzles.title: Blocked or Free?
puzzles.prompt: For each highlighted destination: can the Horse reach it? (Yes/No)
puzzles.setup: Red Ma on e5 with various blockers, 4 destinations highlighted
puzzles.teaches: Rapid leg-block recognition

puzzles.title: Horse Capture
puzzles.prompt: Capture the unprotected Xe with your Horse.
puzzles.setup: Red Ma on c1, Black Xe on d3, no blocker on d1

puzzles.title: Unblock the Horse
puzzles.prompt: Move your Xe to unblock your Horse's path to capture.
puzzles.setup: Red Ma on e5 blocked by Red Xe on e6, target Black piece on f7
puzzles.teaches: Piece coordination — introduced pieces work together

puzzles.title: Horse vs Chariot
puzzles.prompt: Your Horse is attacked by a Chariot. Find the only safe square that also threatens the Chariot back.
puzzles.setup: Tactical puzzle using Ma and Xe interaction

use_it.title: Chariots vs Horses
use_it.setup: Each side gets 2 Xe and 2 Ma. No other pieces.
use_it.objective: Capture all opponent's pieces or force them into a position with no legal moves.
use_it.teaches: How Xe and Ma interact — the Horse's ability to threaten squares the Chariot can't

lessons.lesson_summary: The Horse is tricky but powerful. That leg-block rule is what makes Cờ tướng's Horse unique. When you see 馬 move at the café, watch for what's blocking it.

lessons.title: The Fortress — General and Advisors
lessons.café_context: See that 3x3 grid with diagonal lines on each end of the board? That's the Cung — the palace or fortress. The General (帥/將) and Advisors (仕/士) can never leave it. The General is the piece you must protect at all costs.
learning_objectives: Recognize 帥/將 (General) and 仕/士 (Advisor) characters
Understand the palace boundaries (3x3 grid, 9 intersections)
Understand General moves one step orthogonally within the palace
Understand Advisor moves one step diagonally within the palace
Understand check: when the General is threatened, you MUST respond

content.name: Tướng — General & Sĩ — Advisor
content.movement_diagram: Show palace with General's 4 orthogonal moves and Advisor's 4 diagonal moves highlighted
content.real_world_tip: The General is your King. The Advisors are bodyguards who never leave the fortress. Together they form a defensive unit.
content.cultural_note: Tướng means 'general' — the same word in the game's name Cờ tướng (General's Chess). This piece IS the game.

try_it.title: Defend the Fortress

setup.board: Red: Tướng + 2 Sĩ in palace. Black: 2 Xe approaching.

try_it.objective: Survive 10 moves without getting checkmated. The AI attacks with Chariots.
rules: General and Advisors move only within the palace
Check must be addressed immediately
Player learns defensive coordination
try_it.ai_difficulty: Moderate — AI makes logical attacks but leaves some escape routes

puzzles.title: Escape Check
puzzles.prompt: Your General is in check. Find the safe move.
puzzles.setup: Red Tướng on e1 checked by Black Xe on e7. Sĩ on d2 and f2.
puzzles.teaches: Responding to check by moving the General

puzzles.title: Block the Check
puzzles.prompt: Your General is in check. Use an Advisor to block.
puzzles.setup: Red Tướng on e1 checked by Black Xe on e7. Sĩ available to interpose.
puzzles.teaches: Responding to check by blocking with Advisor

puzzles.title: Palace Boundaries
puzzles.prompt: Tap all intersections the Advisor can legally reach.
puzzles.setup: Sĩ on e2 (center of palace edge)
puzzles.teaches: Advisor stays on diagonals within palace

puzzles.title: Checkmate in One
puzzles.prompt: Deliver checkmate using your Chariot.
puzzles.setup: Red Xe vs Black Tướng + Sĩ in palace. One-move mate available.

puzzles.title: Fortress Coordination
puzzles.prompt: Position your Advisor to protect the General AND block the diagonal.
puzzles.setup: Defensive positioning puzzle

use_it.title: Storm the Fortress
use_it.setup: Red: Tướng + 2 Sĩ + 1 Xe. Black: Tướng + 2 Sĩ + 1 Xe + 1 Ma.
use_it.teaches: Attacking vs defending the palace using all pieces learned so far

lessons.lesson_summary: The palace is the heart of Cờ tướng. Protecting your Tướng while breaking into your opponent's fortress — that's the game. When you watch at the café, notice how players angle their attacks toward the palace.

lessons.title: The Elephant — Guardian of Your Side
lessons.café_context: The discs marked 相 or 象 are Tượng — Elephants. They move exactly two intersections diagonally, but they cannot cross the river in the middle of the board, and they can be blocked at the midpoint of their jump.
learning_objectives: Recognize 相 (red) and 象 (black) characters
Understand the exactly-two-diagonal movement pattern
Understand the river restriction: Elephants never cross the river
Understand the eye-block rule: a piece at the midpoint of the diagonal blocks the move
Understand the river as a board feature that affects multiple pieces

content.name: Tượng — Elephant
content.movement_diagram: Show the 4 possible destinations (2 diagonals) with eye-block points highlighted and river boundary marked
content.real_world_tip: Think of a Bishop that can only move exactly 2 squares diagonally and can't cross the center line. It's purely defensive.
content.cultural_note: 象 (tượng) means elephant. War elephants were real — they defended the rear. This piece does the same.

try_it.title: Elephant Patrol

setup.board: Red: 2 Tượng on starting positions. Black: 2 Ma that cross the river.

try_it.objective: Use your two Elephants to block or capture the invading Horses for 8 moves.
rules: Elephants move exactly 2 intersections diagonally
Cannot cross the river
Eye-block rule enforced with visual indicator
Capture enemy Horses by landing on their intersection
try_it.ai_difficulty: Horses try to penetrate. Player learns Elephant's defensive coverage.

puzzles.title: Elephant's Range
puzzles.prompt: Tap all intersections this Elephant can reach.
puzzles.setup: Red Tượng on c1 with one eye-block present

puzzles.title: Blocked Eye
puzzles.prompt: Can the Elephant reach each highlighted intersection? (Yes/No)
puzzles.setup: Red Tượng on c5 with 2 of 4 eyes blocked

puzzles.title: River Boundary
puzzles.prompt: The enemy Horse is across the river. Can your Elephant capture it?
puzzles.setup: Red Tượng near river, Black Ma just across
puzzles.teaches: Elephants CANNOT cross the river — ever

puzzles.title: Defensive Formation
puzzles.prompt: Position your Elephant to protect both the Advisor and block the enemy Chariot's diagonal approach.

puzzles.title: Clear the Eye
puzzles.prompt: Move your Chariot to unblock your Elephant's path.
puzzles.teaches: Coordination — clearing blocking pieces to enable Elephant movement

use_it.title: Invasion Defense
use_it.setup: Red: Tướng + 2 Sĩ + 2 Tượng + 1 Xe. Black: Tướng + 2 Sĩ + 2 Ma + 1 Xe.
use_it.objective: Checkmate. Red has the defensive advantage with Elephants. Black has mobile Horses.
use_it.teaches: How defensive pieces (Advisor, Elephant) coordinate to protect the General while Chariot attacks

lessons.lesson_summary: Elephants are your defensive wall. They never cross the river, and they can be blocked — but positioned well, they lock down your side of the board. At the café, notice how players rarely move their Elephants early.

lessons.title: The Cannon — The Soul of Cờ tướng
lessons.café_context: This is the piece that makes Cờ tướng its own game. The discs marked 炮 or 砲 are Pháo — Cannons. They MOVE like a Chariot (any distance along rank or file). But to CAPTURE, they must jump over exactly one piece — friend or foe. That middle piece is called the 'screen.'
learning_objectives: Recognize 炮 (red) and 砲 (black) characters
Understand that Cannon moves like Chariot when NOT capturing
Understand the screen rule: to capture, must jump over exactly one intervening piece
Understand the screen can be friendly or enemy
Understand Cannon's power decreases as pieces are traded (fewer screens available)

content.name: Pháo — Cannon
content.movement_diagram: Two diagrams: (1) movement without capture — same as Chariot. (2) capture — show the screen piece and the target beyond it
content.real_world_tip: This piece has no equivalent in Western chess. It's the signature piece of Cờ tướng. Master this and you understand the game.
content.cultural_note: Pháo means cannon or artillery. The character 炮 originally meant catapult — a weapon that launches projectiles OVER obstacles. The movement rule mirrors this perfectly.

try_it.title: Cannon Target Practice

setup.board: Red: 2 Pháo. Board has 6 neutral 'screen' pieces and 4 black targets.

try_it.objective: Capture all 4 targets using screen jumps. Screens are not removed when used.
rules: Cannon moves freely along ranks and files (like Chariot) when not capturing
To capture: must jump over exactly one piece (the screen) to land on the target
Screens remain on the board
Find the sequence to capture all 4 targets
try_it.success_criteria: All 4 targets captured. Bonus for fewest total moves.

puzzles.title: Cannon Capture
puzzles.prompt: Use the screen to capture the enemy piece.
puzzles.setup: Red Pháo on a1, neutral piece on a5, Black piece on a8

puzzles.title: No Screen, No Capture
puzzles.prompt: Can the Cannon capture each highlighted piece? (Yes/No)
puzzles.setup: Red Pháo on e1, various pieces on e-file — some with screens, some without
puzzles.teaches: Without exactly one screen, Cannon cannot capture

puzzles.title: Cannon Checkmate
puzzles.prompt: Deliver checkmate using your Cannon. Find the screen.
puzzles.setup: Red Pháo on e7, Red piece as screen on e4, Black Tướng on e1 in palace

puzzles.title: Create a Screen
puzzles.prompt: Move a piece into position to serve as a screen for your Cannon's capture.
puzzles.teaches: Actively creating screens through piece coordination

puzzles.title: Double Cannon
puzzles.prompt: One Cannon serves as the other's screen. Find the checkmate.
puzzles.setup: Two Red Pháo on the same file with Black Tướng at the end
puzzles.teaches: Classic Cờ tướng tactic — Cannons supporting each other

use_it.title: Artillery Assault
use_it.setup: Red: Tướng + 2 Sĩ + 2 Tượng + 2 Pháo. Black: Tướng + 2 Sĩ + 2 Tượng + 2 Xe.
use_it.objective: Checkmate. Cannons vs Chariots — learn how Cannons use defensive pieces as screens to attack.
use_it.teaches: The Cannon's paradox: your own defensive pieces become weapons when a Cannon is behind them

lessons.lesson_summary: The Pháo is what makes Cờ tướng unique. Watch a game at the café and you'll see experienced players constantly maneuvering pieces to create or remove screens. If you understand the Cannon, you understand Cờ tướng.

lessons.title: The Soldier — Small But Brave
lessons.café_context: The discs marked 兵 or 卒 are Tốt — Soldiers. They start weak, moving only one step forward. But once they cross the river, they gain the ability to move sideways too. They never retreat.
learning_objectives: Recognize 兵 (red) and 卒 (black) characters
Understand Soldiers move one step forward only (before crossing river)
Understand Soldiers gain sideways movement after crossing the river
Understand Soldiers never move backward
Understand the river as a power-up boundary for Soldiers

content.name: Tốt — Soldier
content.movement_diagram: Two diagrams: (1) Before river — forward only. (2) After river — forward or sideways.
content.real_world_tip: Unlike chess pawns, Soldiers don't move diagonally to capture. They always move and capture the same way. And there's no promotion — once a Soldier reaches the back rank, it can only move sideways.
content.cultural_note: 卒 (tốt) means foot soldier. 兵 (binh) means soldier/army. Five Soldiers per side — they form your front line, and crossed-river Soldiers are surprisingly dangerous.

try_it.title: Cross the River

setup.board: 5 Red Tốt on starting positions vs 5 Black Tốt on starting positions

try_it.objective: Get 3 Soldiers across the river before your opponent does.
rules: Soldiers move one step forward before the river
After crossing, can also move one step sideways
Capture by moving onto opponent's intersection
No backward movement ever
try_it.ai_difficulty: Easy — AI demonstrates basic Soldier tactics

puzzles.title: Soldier's Reach
puzzles.prompt: Tap all intersections this crossed-river Soldier can reach.
puzzles.setup: Red Tốt on e6 (across river)
puzzles.teaches: Crossed-river Soldier has 3 possible moves (forward, left, right)

puzzles.title: Soldier Checkmate
puzzles.prompt: Deliver checkmate with a Soldier.
puzzles.setup: Red Tốt crossed river, near Black Tướng in palace with limited escape
puzzles.teaches: Crossed-river Soldiers are genuinely dangerous to the General

puzzles.title: Soldier as Screen
puzzles.prompt: Advance your Soldier to serve as a screen for your Cannon's checkmate.
puzzles.teaches: Soldiers make excellent Cannon screens

puzzles.title: No Retreat
puzzles.prompt: Your Soldier is on the back rank. Find the only useful move.
puzzles.setup: Red Tốt on e9 — can only move sideways
puzzles.teaches: End-of-board Soldiers are limited but still useful

puzzles.title: Soldier Army
puzzles.prompt: You have 3 crossed-river Soldiers. Which one should advance to create a mating threat?
puzzles.teaches: Multiple Soldiers coordinate powerfully

use_it.title: All Arms
use_it.setup: Full piece set MINUS Cannons for both sides.
use_it.objective: Checkmate. Practice with all pieces learned except the Cannon.
use_it.teaches: Near-complete game coordination. Cannons removed to keep complexity manageable for one more lesson.

lessons.lesson_summary: Soldiers look weak but crossed-river Tốt win games. Experienced players at the café will push Soldiers aggressively. Now you know why.

lessons.title: The Full Game — Flying Generals and You
lessons.café_context: You now know every piece. There's one last rule: the Flying General. The two Generals can never face each other on the same file with nothing between them. Think of it as a staring contest — if they're looking at each other with no pieces in the way, the position is illegal. This rule creates tactics you won't see in any other game.
learning_objectives: Understand the Flying General rule and how it restricts movement
Understand how Flying General creates tactical pins and attacks
Play a complete game of Cờ tướng with all rules enforced
Recognize the opening setup and standard first moves
Identify common opening patterns seen in café play

content.title: Flying General Rule (Đối mặt Tướng)
content.diagram: Show two Generals on same file with open path — illegal. Show same file with piece between — legal.
content.explanation: If both Generals are on the same file, at least one piece must be between them. This means pieces standing between the Generals are effectively 'pinned' — moving them would expose the illegal face-off.
content.tactical_tip: This creates the most unique tactic in Cờ tướng: using your General as an attacker. If you clear the file between the Generals, your General 'attacks' the enemy General through the empty file.

try_it.title: Your First Full Game
try_it.objective: Play a complete game against the AI with all rules enforced.
rules: All piece movement rules from Lessons 1-6
Flying General rule enforced
Check, checkmate, and stalemate (loss) enforced
'Why illegal?' panel active for any blocked moves
try_it.ai_difficulty: Easy — AI makes reasonable but beatable moves. Prioritizes teaching moments.
coach_features: Highlight when Flying General rule prevents a move
Post-move hint if player misses a significant tactic

puzzles.title: Flying General Pin
puzzles.prompt: Move the piece between the Generals to win material — the Flying General rule pins your opponent.
puzzles.teaches: Using Flying General as a tactical weapon

puzzles.title: General as Attacker
puzzles.prompt: Clear the file to 'attack' with your General. Find checkmate.
puzzles.teaches: The General can deliver checkmate through the Flying General rule

puzzles.title: Double Cannon Mate
puzzles.prompt: Classic Cờ tướng checkmate pattern. Set up the double Cannon.
puzzles.teaches: Signature mating pattern

puzzles.title: Horse and Cannon Mate
puzzles.prompt: The Horse controls the escape squares while the Cannon delivers check.
puzzles.teaches: Piece coordination for checkmate

puzzles.title: Chariot Sacrifice
puzzles.prompt: Sacrifice your Chariot to force checkmate in 2 moves.
puzzles.teaches: Tactical sacrifice — advanced beginner concept

use_it.title: Rematch
use_it.setup: Standard starting position. AI slightly stronger than first game.
use_it.objective: Play again with confidence. Win or learn.

lessons.lesson_summary: You now know every rule of Cờ tướng. You can read the pieces, you know the Vietnamese names, and you understand what's happening at the table next to you. Sit down and play.

spaced_repetition_system.description: Puzzles from earlier lessons resurface in later sessions based on a simplified Leitner-style system.

implementation.storage: localStorage with fallback for Safari private browsing
implementation.algorithm: Modified Leitner box system with 4 levels
rules: Correct answer on first attempt: advance one box
Incorrect answer: return to Box 1
Each lesson session includes 2-3 review puzzles from earlier lessons
Review puzzles are selected from the lowest box number first

ui_integration_notes.lesson_mode_entry: Main menu button: 'Learn Cờ tướng' — opens lesson selector

lesson_selector.display: 7 lessons shown as a vertical path/journey (not a grid)
lesson_selector.locked_lessons: Lessons unlock sequentially after completing the previous lesson's try_it mini-game
lesson_selector.progress_indicators: Stars or checkmarks for see_it, try_it, test_it, use_it completion

piece_card_access.in_lesson: Shown as the first step of each lesson
piece_card_access.in_game: Long-press any piece during play to see its card (existing feature)
piece_card_access.in_puzzle: Tap help icon to see cards for pieces in the current puzzle

three_layer_label_display.primary: Chinese character (what they see on the physical disc)
three_layer_label_display.secondary: Vietnamese name (what they hear at the café)
three_layer_label_display.always_visible: All three layers visible on piece cards and in piece info panels
three_layer_label_display.in_game_pieces: Show character by default. Toggle available for Vietnamese name overlay.

bottom_sheet_integration.during_lessons: Bottom sheet shows current objective and hint button
bottom_sheet_integration.during_puzzles: Bottom sheet shows puzzle prompt and 'Show solution' after 2 failed attempts
bottom_sheet_integration.during_games: 'Why illegal?' panel appears here when a blocked move is attempted

audio_future.status: Not in current sprint — stubbed for future implementation
audio_future.planned: Vietnamese pronunciation of piece names using Web Audio API or pre-recorded clips
audio_future.trigger: Tap the Vietnamese name on any piece card to hear pronunciation

café_play_features.description: Features specifically designed for the expat-at-a-café use case

quick_reference_mode.trigger: Shake phone or tap floating button during any screen
quick_reference_mode.content: Overlay showing all 7 piece types with character → Vietnamese name → movement summary
quick_reference_mode.purpose: Player is watching a live game and needs to quickly identify a piece they see on the physical board

physical_board_companion.trigger: 'Watch mode' button — puts the app in a passive reference state
physical_board_companion.content: Large display of piece cards, swipeable. No game logic running.
physical_board_companion.purpose: Reference guide while watching or playing on a physical board

share_feature.content: Shareable card: 'I just learned the [piece name] in Cờ tướng! 🏯' with link to app
share_feature.purpose: Facebook expat group engagement and organic growth

dependencies: Complete game engine (all piece movement and rules — DONE)
Board rendering and touch interaction — DONE
week_1: Implement lesson data loader (parse this JSON into app state)
Build lesson flow UI: see_it → try_it → test_it → use_it progression
Implement piece cards with three-layer labeling
Build mini-game framework (limited piece sets on existing board engine)
week_2: Build spaced repetition tracker (Leitner boxes in localStorage)
Implement lesson selector with progress tracking
Polish, test on physical iPhone, deploy
